rationale: The citing paper studies gender performance gaps in business-school courses using exogenous student–instructor assignment and grade/survey mechanisms, whereas the cited paper develops a masculine/feminine brand personality scale for consumer-brand, advertising, and brand-extension contexts. Based on the notes, there is only a broad “gender” overlap and no clear shared method, dataset, or direct theoretical dependency, and the evidence includes only a single DOI match with no contextual citation text.
